context.projectOverview: Sprint 5 iter 1 passed 7/9. Two narrow gaps to close in iter 2.

items.problem: Red Flags table has 7 'About to' bullets; contract requires >=8. Rationalization Prevention table has 6 data rows; contract requires >=7. Sprint 3 reference (bober-evaluator.md): 10 Red Flags, 9 Rationalization rows.
items.fix: Append AT LEAST 1 more Red Flag and 1 more Rationalization row. Make them code-review-specific (not bober-evaluator copies). Suggested entries: 'About to file a finding for pre-existing code that the sprint didn't change' (Red Flag); a Rationalization row like 'just this once / I'll review the whole file even though only X changed' → 'Stick to the diff. Pre-existing code is out of scope.'

items.problem: All 3 contract assertions are structurally hollow — tests pass but never exercise pipeline.ts. (a) mocks runCodeReviewer and calls the mock directly + asserts mock was called (tautological); (b) hand-builds markdown bypassing renderReviewMarkdown then calls saveReview directly; (c) replicates pipeline.ts try/catch inline rather than calling runSprintCycle. Pipeline.ts L370-382 (the actual advisory error handling) is untested.
items.fix: Rewrite as follows: (a) vi.mock the code-reviewer-agent module so runCodeReviewer is a spy; call runSprintCycle (or the smallest pipeline slice that contains the if (evaluation.passed) branch) with an evaluator stub returning {passed:true, ...}; assert the spy was called with the contract+evaluation values that the pipeline propagated. (b) Either: import renderReviewMarkdown directly with a ReviewResult fixture and assert the returned string contains all 6 H1/H2 headings in order — that's a unit test of the renderer. OR: with the full runCodeReviewer mocked to return a ReviewResult shape, run pipeline and read the written file to assert it contains the 6 headings. (c) Make the runCodeReviewer spy throw; call runSprintCycle; assert (1) pipeline did NOT throw, (2) the returned sprint state has status='passed' (or whatever the success status is — check pipeline.ts), (3) logger.warn was called. This is the ACTUAL coverage of pipeline.ts L370-382.

auxiliaryFindings: All other 7 criteria pass independently. Do NOT touch what works.
Existing 589/4 test state must not regress. New iteration must end with 589/4 (or +N pass / 4 skipped if tests grow).
src/orchestrator/pipeline.ts LOCALIZATION still holds — only ADD test coverage. Do not restructure production code.

notes: Read the eval result at .bober/eval-results/eval-sprint-spec-20260524-bober-vision-5-1.json for the full evidence and reproduction commands. Read the original briefing at .bober/briefings/sprint-spec-20260524-bober-vision-5-briefing.md (still relevant). New commit message: 'bober(sprint-5 iter 2): code-review table thresholds + real pipeline.ts integration test coverage'.
